en:
# Sentra XML Protocol Specification

This runtime uses Sentra XML for structured tool interaction.

## Core Tags
- `<sentra-input>`: system-provided read-only context for the current round.
- `<sentra-tools>`: assistant-generated tool invocation block (exactly one per response when calling tools).
- `<sentra-result>` / `<sentra-result-group>`: system-generated execution results (read-only).

## `<sentra-input>` Structure
Typical layout:
- `<current_messages>
    <sentra-message>` with `<message><segment ...>` entries (primary task signal).
- Optional context blocks: `<sentra-pending-messages>`, `<sentra-history-messages>`, `<sentra-tool-results>`, `<sentra-rag-context>`.

Use `type + data` in each segment (for example text/image/file/etc.).

## `<sentra-tools>` Rules
- Exactly one block per response.
- Inside each `<parameter>`, use typed nodes only:
  - `<string>`, `<number>`, `<boolean>`, `<null>`, `<array>`, `<object>`.
- `<array>` children must be typed nodes only.
- `<object>` children must be `<parameter name="...">VALUE</parameter>`.
- Do not place raw JSON objects/arrays directly in parameter text.

## XML Escaping
Inside `<string>`, escape `<`, `>`, `&`, `"`, and `'`.

## Result Group Meta Fields
- In `<sentra-result-group>`, use child tags `<step_group_id>`, `<group_size>`, `<order_step_ids>`, `<status>`.
- `step_group_id` is internal execution id, not chat route `group_id`.

## Strictness
- No markdown fences around XML.
- No extra explanatory text around `<sentra-tools>`.
- Parameter names must match schema exactly.
- Never fabricate `<sentra-result>` content.

# Sentra XML Protocol Specification

This runtime uses Sentra XML for structured tool interaction.

## Core Tags
- `<sentra-input>`: system-provided read-only context for the current round.
- `<sentra-tools>`: assistant-generated tool invocation block (exactly one per response when calling tools).
- `<sentra-result>` / `<sentra-result-group>`: system-generated execution results (read-only).

## `<sentra-input>` Structure
Typical layout:
- `<current_messages>
    <sentra-message>` with `<message><segment ...>` entries (primary task signal).
- Optional context blocks: `<sentra-pending-messages>`, `<sentra-history-messages>`, `<sentra-tool-results>`, `<sentra-rag-context>`.

Use `type + data` in each segment (for example text/image/file/etc.).

## `<sentra-tools>` Rules
- Exactly one block per response.
- Inside each `<parameter>`, use typed nodes only:
  - `<string>`, `<number>`, `<boolean>`, `<null>`, `<array>`, `<object>`.
- `<array>` children must be typed nodes only.
- `<object>` children must be `<parameter name="...">VALUE</parameter>`.
- Do not place raw JSON objects/arrays directly in parameter text.

## XML Escaping
Inside `<string>`, escape `<`, `>`, `&`, `"`, and `'`.

## Result Group Meta Fields
- In `<sentra-result-group>`, use child tags `<step_group_id>`, `<group_size>`, `<order_step_ids>`, `<status>`.
- `step_group_id` is internal execution id, not chat route `group_id`.

## Strictness
- No markdown fences around XML.
- No extra explanatory text around `<sentra-tools>`.
- Parameter names must match schema exactly.
- Never fabricate `<sentra-result>` content.
